LJCCreateFileChangesLib5.summary: Contains code to create the FileChanges file.

ConstructorParams.summary: Initializes an object instance with the supplied values.

remarks.para: Filespecs are retrieved for each IncludeFilter.

First, filespecs are retrieved from the Target codeline.
A Source filespec is created based on the target filespec but in the the source codeline.
A "Delete" command is written to the "Changes" file to delete the target file if there is no associated source file of that name.
This keeps the folders synched.
However, this behavior must be managed carefully to prevent losing code.

Next, filespecs are retrieved from the Source codeline.
A Target filespec is created based on the source filespec but in the the target codeline.
A "Copy" command is written to the "Changes" file to add the source file if there is no associated target file of that name.
A "Copy" command is also written to the "Changes" file to update the target file if it is different from the source file.

IncludeFilters.summary: Gets or sets the included file filters.

remarks.para: A list of file patterns using wild cards separated with the "Vertical Bar" (pipe) character. For example: "*.cs|*.sln|*.csproj |*.cmd"
Files that match one of these file patterns will be included in the Code Backup.
Files will be copied to the target folder if they do not exist there or the source file has changed.

Files will be deleted from the target folder if they do not exist in the source folder. This keeps the folders synched.
However, this behavior must be managed carefully to prevent losing code.

IncludeMissingTargetFolders.summary: Gets or sets the include missing target folders flag.

remarks.para: The standard behavior is to only copy files where the target folder already exists.
The standard behavior uses this implicit way of controlling which folders get backed up instead of having to explicitly list the source folders to be included.
When a new source folder is created, it must be copied to or created in the target codeline to be included in the backup.
Setting the IncludeMissingTargetFolders property to true overrides the standard behavior and will create missing target folders.

SkipFiles.summary: Gets or sets the skip file list.

remarks.para: A list of file patterns using wild cards separated with the "Vertical Bar" (pipe) character. For example: "Build*.cmd|Example*.cs"
Files that match one of these file patterns will not be included in the Code Backup.
